mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals an abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when either series trends independently.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a low-inflation environment confirms genuine supply-side capacity expansion without triggering monetary tightening, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when inflation exceeds 2.5% despite rising industrial production, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 55% in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic activity and wage growth, which directly boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently fails to predict positive forward returns, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate market repricing of future central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The market fails to price in tightening expectations, with forward 5-day returns remaining positive in >65% of high-inflation trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP and personal consumption expenditures confirms a synchronized demand expansion, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive power when GDP growth is driven solely by inventory accumulation rather than consumption, or forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment signals a broad supply-demand mismatch and economic contraction, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of cases when both series move in the predicted direction, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.
